question_set.name: Danzell
question_set.source_note: Paraphrased local research mapping derived from the official IASME preparation question set. Verbatim question text is intentionally not reproduced.

questions.short_paraphrase: State the legal organisation name for the assessment certificate.
Identify the organisation type or legal form.
Declare the workforce size, including relevant workers with access to organisational data.
planned_evidence_paths: HR or identity directory count with caveats

questions.short_paraphrase: Provide the primary legal entity registration identifier.
Record the registered address for the legal organisation.
Record operational locations that differ from the registered address.
State whether multiple legal entities are included in the certification scope.
List additional legal entities included in the assessment scope.
Select the organisation's main business sector.
Provide the organisation's public web presence if one exists.
State whether this is a first application or a renewal.
Record the main reasons for seeking certification.
Identify a commercial contracting organisation where relevant.
Identify government contract context where relevant.
Identify grant authority context where relevant.
Identify regulator context where relevant.
Explain any other certification motivation.
Confirm the requirements document has been read.
Record whether advice was received from an assured Cyber Advisor.
Record breach follow-up consent preference.
Record how the organisation heard about Cyber Essentials.
Record consent to be contacted for research.
Record whether the organisation uses the NCSC Early Warning service.
State whether the scope is the whole organisation or a defined subset.
Describe the assessment scope where only part of the organisation is included.
Describe excluded networks created as subsets.
Explain the mechanism used to create any subset boundary.
Confirm whether in-scope networks operate at the declared locations.
Record whether each organisation site has internet connectivity.
Describe site-to-site connectivity where direct site internet is absent.
List networks included in the assessment scope.
Record the number of home or remote workers.
Describe how remote workers access organisational data and services.
planned_evidence_paths: VPN, ZTNA, or SaaS access inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: List in-scope routers, firewalls, or equivalent network-boundary equipment.
planned_evidence_paths: firewall, router, and cloud firewall inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: List equipment used to define any partial-scope subset.
Summarise laptops, desktops, and virtual desktops in scope.
planned_evidence_paths: Intune, MDM, Defender for Endpoint, device inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: Summarise thin clients in scope.
Summarise servers, hypervisors, and virtual desktop infrastructure in scope.
Summarise tablets and mobile devices in scope.
List third-party cloud services used for organisational data or services.
Identify the internal person responsible for in-scope IT systems.
Confirm eligibility conditions for included cyber insurance.
Record whether the organisation opts into included insurance.
Provide contact details for insurance documents if opting in.
Confirm firewall protection exists between in-scope systems and the internet.
planned_evidence_paths: NSG, firewall, router, endpoint firewall inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm software firewalls are enabled on end-user and server devices.
planned_evidence_paths: Intune, MDM, Defender for Endpoint, local firewall inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: Explain any operating systems where software firewalls are unavailable.
Confirm default firewall or router administrative passwords have been changed.
Describe how firewall administrative passwords are changed.
Identify the password or passwordless protection model for firewall administration.
Confirm firewall passwords are changed after suspected compromise.
Confirm a process exists for managing firewall rule changes.
Confirm firewall rules have been reviewed within the expected period.
Confirm unauthenticated inbound connections are blocked by default.
planned_evidence_paths: cloud firewall default action, NSG effective rules

questions.short_paraphrase: Describe approval and documentation for allowed inbound connections.
Confirm whether firewall configuration interfaces are internet-accessible.
Confirm documented business need for any internet-accessible firewall administration.
Confirm remote firewall administration is protected by MFA or trusted-source restrictions.
planned_evidence_paths: firewall admin source allow-list and identity controls

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm unused software and services are removed or disabled across devices and cloud services.
planned_evidence_paths: Defender for Endpoint, Intune, software inventory, cloud service inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm only necessary and active user accounts exist across devices and cloud services.
planned_evidence_paths: Entra users, SaaS users, local device accounts

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm default passwords have been changed on user and administrator accounts for devices.
planned_evidence_paths: device configuration policy, local account audit

questions.short_paraphrase: Identify externally reachable services that provide access to non-public data.
planned_evidence_paths: public endpoint inventory, app gateway, app service access restrictions

questions.short_paraphrase: Identify the authentication protection used for externally reachable non-public services.
planned_evidence_paths: Entra app authentication, WAF or app auth configuration

questions.short_paraphrase: Describe password-change process for externally reachable services after compromise.
Identify brute-force protection used when MFA is not used for external services.
planned_evidence_paths: account lockout policy, WAF throttling, Entra smart lockout

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm automatic execution of imported or downloaded files is disabled.
Confirm user-present devices require a locking mechanism.
Identify the device unlock method and brute-force protection.
Confirm operating systems and firmware are supported and receive security fixes.
planned_evidence_paths: VM image metadata, Defender recommendations, endpoint inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm application software is supported and receives vulnerability fixes.
planned_evidence_paths: software inventory, Defender for Endpoint, SaaS inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: List browsers and versions in use.
List malware protection products and versions in use.
List email applications installed on devices and servers.
List office or productivity applications used to create organisational data.
Confirm whether any in-scope software or cloud services are unsupported or unlicensed.
planned_evidence_paths: software licence inventory, SaaS licence inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: List unsupported or unlicensed software or cloud services where present.
Confirm high-risk operating system and firmware fixes are applied within the required time window.
planned_evidence_paths: Azure Update Manager, Defender vulnerability assessment, endpoint patch telemetry

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm operating system automatic updates are enabled where possible.
planned_evidence_paths: Intune, Azure Update Manager, MDM update policy

questions.short_paraphrase: Describe update process where automatic operating system updates are not used.
Confirm high-risk application fixes are applied within the required time window.
planned_evidence_paths: software inventory, Defender for Endpoint vulnerability management

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm application automatic updates are enabled where possible.
Describe update process where automatic application updates are not used.
Confirm unsupported software has been removed from devices.
Explain how unsupported software is isolated where there is a business need to retain it.
Describe the approval process for creating user accounts.
Confirm user and administrator accounts use unique credentials.
planned_evidence_paths: Entra user inventory, shared account detection

questions.short_paraphrase: Describe how leaver accounts are removed or disabled.
planned_evidence_paths: Entra stale user and sign-in telemetry

questions.short_paraphrase: Describe how least privilege is applied to staff access.
planned_evidence_paths: role assignment review, PIM, access reviews

questions.short_paraphrase: Describe the process for granting administrator-level access.
planned_evidence_paths: PIM, approval workflow, access review records

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm administrative tasks use separate administrator accounts.
Explain how administrator accounts are prevented from everyday use.
Confirm administrator account holders are tracked.
Confirm administrative access is reviewed regularly.
planned_evidence_paths: Entra access reviews, PIM review history

questions.short_paraphrase: Identify brute-force protection for password-based systems.
planned_evidence_paths: Entra smart lockout, account lockout policy, app lockout settings

questions.short_paraphrase: Identify technical controls used to maintain password quality.
planned_evidence_paths: Entra authentication methods and password policy

questions.short_paraphrase: Explain how users are encouraged to use unique and strong passwords.
planned_evidence_paths: security awareness and password manager policy evidence

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm there is a process for suspected password or account compromise.
Confirm whether all cloud services provide MFA capability.
planned_evidence_paths: SaaS inventory, cloud service MFA capability catalogue

questions.short_paraphrase: List cloud services that do not offer MFA where applicable.
Confirm MFA is applied to cloud service administrators where available.
planned_evidence_paths: Conditional Access policies, credential registration details

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm MFA is applied to cloud service users where available.
planned_evidence_paths: Conditional Access policies, credential registration details

questions.short_paraphrase: Identify malware protection approach for in-scope end-user devices.
planned_evidence_paths: Defender for Endpoint, Intune, MDM, app control inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm anti-malware updates and execution blocking are configured where anti-malware is used.
planned_evidence_paths: Defender for Endpoint health, AV policy inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm malicious website blocking or warning is configured where anti-malware is used.
planned_evidence_paths: Defender for Endpoint web protection, browser policy inventory

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm unsigned applications are blocked where application allow-listing or app-store restrictions are used.
planned_evidence_paths: Intune, application control, MDM restriction policy

questions.short_paraphrase: Confirm approved-application controls and approved application lists are maintained where allow-listing is used.
planned_evidence_paths: approved app list, MDM app policy, application control policy
